question:
What is the evaluation criteria for IoT Networks,
  Architectures and Applications?
answer: The evaluation criteria for IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications involves a combination of mid-term and end-term evaluations, along with other components, with a minimum of 40% marks required to become eligible for grading. This is based on the relative grading method defined in the academic regulations of the university, as outlined in the Evaluation Summary section of the course handouts. The evaluation components include a mid-term exam (30%), lab assignments (20%), Coursera certification (10%), and an end-term project (40%), as detailed in the Evaluation Summary section of both IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications-CSE 2023 Batch.pdf and IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications-CSE 2023 Batch.pdf.

retrieved_chunks.source: IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications-CSE 2023 Batch.pdf
retrieved_chunks.text:
Course: IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications
Course Code: CSE3722
Credits: 03 (L-D-P: 3-0-0)
Section: Evaluation Summary
Section Type: evaluation

The final grade will be based on the marks/ grades obtained in the mid-term and end-term evaluations along with other components as defined in the evaluation plan/table. The relative grading method defined in the academic regulations of the university will be followed to grade the students. The student has to secure aminimum of 40% of marks after completing all the assessments in the following table to become eligible for grading. Evaluation Component Weightage (%) Evaluation Schedule/Timeline Evaluation Type/Remarks CO mapping Mid-Term Exam 30% As per academic calendar Closed book CO1, CO2 Lab Assignments 20% Continuous Based on lab experiments CO1, CO2, CO3 Coursera Certification 10% To be completed by 2nd/3rd week of April Closed book CO1, CO2, CO3 End-Term (Project-Based) 40% 1st week of May Demonstration, viva and report CO1, CO2, CO3 Experiential Learning Component: The lab assignments and end-term project will facilitate 60% experiential learning.

retrieved_chunks.source: IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications-CSE 2023 Batch.pdf
retrieved_chunks.text:
Course: IoT Networks, Architectures and Applications
Course Code: CSE3722
Credits: 03 (L-D-P: 3-0-0)
Section: Course Coordinator
Section Type: faculty

Dr. Sandeep Kumar
Email: sandeep.kumar@bmu.edu.in
Contact No: +91-9133872511
Office: 67, IV Floor, E2 Building

Aim of the course : This course is designed to provide students with solid technical knowledge and skills to
understand the Internet of Things (IoT) systems. The beginning portion focuses on the basic concepts,
architectures, applications and design challenges of the internet of things. The various topics on the IoT
protocols, gateway, computing layers, security and wireless sensing have been covered in the middle portion
of this course. The remaining part takes up the IoT circuits, embedded systems, case studies and a number of
online IoT platforms.
Course Outcomes (CO): At the end of the course the students should be able to do the following:
Course Outcome Statement Mapped
Component
Associated Activities / Assessment
CO1 Understand the basic
concepts, network
architectures, applications and
design challenges in the
internet of things.
Employability  Intensive theory sessions.
 In-class tool demonstrations
 Industry case studies etc.
CO2 Analyse the functionality of
various computing paradigms,
sensor networks, IoT protocols
and security aspects.
Skill Development  Hands-on practical sessions in labs.
 Experiential learning on the simulation
platforms e.g. WokWi, Proteus, CupCarbon,
MATLAB Simulink etc.
 Data communication to the Android Apps
and cloud platforms.
CO3 Apply the acquired knowledge
to design IoT circuits and
systems.
Entrepreneurship,
Skill Development
 Getting familiar with cloud services such as
Blynk, ThingSpeak, HiveMQ, CloudMQTT,
Bolt, PushBullet etc.
 Design and development of the prototypes
based on the real world applications.

Prerequisite: if any
1. Basic knowledge of microcontroller boards.
2. Programming in Python and C.
